message:
feat(wasm): add WebAssembly polyfill for QuickJS runtime (bd-3w08)

Implement PiWasm: a wasmtime-backed WebAssembly polyfill that provides
globalThis.WebAssembly inside the QuickJS sandbox, enabling JS extensions
to use WebAssembly modules (e.g., Emscripten-compiled code) despite
QuickJS lacking native WebAssembly support.

Architecture:
- Native Rust functions (__pi_wasm_*) handle compile/instantiate/call
- JS polyfill wraps them into the standard WebAssembly namespace
- Memory synced as ArrayBuffer snapshots (wasmtime → JS) via getter
- WasmBridgeState manages engine, modules, instances, and memory limits

Integration:
- New src/pi_wasm.rs module (1001 lines) gated behind wasm-host feature
- Registered in src/lib.rs with cfg(feature = "wasm-host")
- Injected into QuickJS global scope in extensions_js.rs PiJsRuntime init
- Added `wat` dev-dependency for WebAssembly text format in tests

The polyfill enforces capability constraints: all side-effectful imports
route through connectors, and memory growth is bounded by policy/budgets.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
